pattern: Precompute row and column prefix sums, then for each candidate side length from large to small test every top-left position, comparing the two diagonal sums against the shared row and column sums.
hints: Try side lengths from the largest possible down to 1, returning the first k for which some k by k subgrid is magic.
Prefix sums over rows and columns let you get any row segment or column segment sum in constant time.
A subgrid is magic when all its row sums, all its column sums, and both diagonals equal one common value.

edge_inputs.description: largest magic square has side three
no 2x2 is magic so answer is one

stress.description: random grids with small values so magic subsquares appear sometimes
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    m = rng.randint(1, 12)
    n = rng.randint(1, 12)
    return [[[rng.randint(1, 4) for _ in range(n)] for _ in range(m)]]
stress.note: low value range raises magic chances

stress.description: constant grids of varying size where the whole grid is magic
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    k = rng.randint(1, 12)
    v = rng.randint(1, 1000000)
    return [[[v] * k for _ in range(k)]]
